description: Severe low-pressure system (Storm Zeynep) tracking northeast across the North Sea. Sustained winds forecast at 55–70 knots, significant wave height 4–6 m. Hamburg port authority has issued precautionary vessel traffic restriction for vessels over 200 m LOA.

Dense advection fog over the Elbe estuary and outer Hamburg anchorage. Visibility dropping below 0.2 nm at anchorage buoys. Elbe pilot authority has suspended inbound convoy movements pending visibility improvement.
Persistent northwesterly gale force 8–9 Beaufort across Hamburg approaches and outer Elbe. Berth operations at Eurogate and HHLA reduced to 60% capacity. Crane operations suspended above 20 m/s.

Residual North Atlantic swell propagating into the German Bight following the earlier storm system. Significant wave height 2.8–3.4 m at outer roads. Pilot boarding operations temporarily suspended; vessels at anchor reporting 12-second period swell.

Storm-force gusts (Beaufort 10) expected along the Nieuwe Waterweg. Port of Rotterdam traffic centre has issued precautionary speed and draught restrictions. Container vessel handling at ECT and APM terminals delayed by 6–12 hours.

Reduced visibility conditions (0.3–0.5 nm) due to combined fog and industrial haze over the Rotterdam outer anchorage and Maasvlakte approaches. Vessel Traffic Services Rotterdam has issued precautionary caution for inbound vessels.

region: North Sea route (Dover Strait – German Bight)
description: Active depression tracking from the Atlantic through the Dover Strait toward the German Bight. Gale warnings issued for sea areas Thames, Humber, German Bight. Vessels on the North Sea route should expect Force 8–10 conditions and possible 12–24 hour route delays.

Persistent long-period swell (height 2.0–2.5 m, period 14 s) on the North Sea transit corridor following North Atlantic storm system. Conditions are navigable but may reduce average vessel speeds by 10–15% and extend transit times.
